name: OmopOrganizationCareSiteView
title: Organization → care_site (FHIR-flat, stage 1)
description:
Each FHIR Organization maps to one OMOP care_site row representing a healthcare facility or department. Organization.name becomes care_site_name, Organization.type maps to place_of_service_concept_id via OrganizationType vocabulary, and Organization.address resolves to a location row linked via location_id. FHIR Location can also contribute care_site rows in some implementations (see Location__care_site edge). FHIR Organization.partOf hierarchy is flattened -- each level becomes a separate care_site row with no parent linkage.

Stage 1 of FHIR→OMOP: produce a flat row keeping source codes per vocabulary. Stage 2 joins vocab.concept to resolve concept_ids.

column.name: id
column.description: FHIR Organization.id; surrogate OMOP key derived (hash/sequence) downstream.

extension.valueString: surrogate key from hash/sequence/lookup of FHIR id

column.name: care_site_name
column.description: The name of the care site. Truncate to 255 characters if needed.

column.name: place_of_service
column.description: High-level characterization of the care site. Should be a concept from the Visit domain. omoponfhir maps via OmopConceptMapping enum (12 codes). 0 if unmapped.

extension.valueString: resolve CONCEPT reference; downstream maps to place_of_service_concept_id

column.name: address_id
column.description: Resolved from Organization.address[0] via address lookup-or-create pattern (omoponfhir AddressUtil). fhir-to-omop-demo sets null from Organization; relies on Location resource to populate via a second care_site row. OMOP supports one location per care_site.

extension.valueString: resolve LOCATION reference; downstream maps to location_id

column.name: managing_organization_identifier
column.description: Identifier for traceability and deduplication. Falls back to Organization.name or Organization.id if no identifier present.

column.name: place_of_service_text
column.description: Raw organization type code or text before vocabulary mapping.
